page_summary: Page showing two 3x3x3 magic cubes (each with magic sum 666), separated by paragraphs of a constructed geometric/runic script, with Hebrew labels 'והדברים 2' and 'והדברים 1' in red.
text_blocks: First block of constructed geometric script (approx. 30+ glyphs, two lines) with red label 'והדברים • 2' centered above it.
Second block of constructed geometric script (approx. 30+ glyphs, two lines) with red label 'והדברים • 1' centered above it.

colored_text.approximate_position: centered between the top magic cube and the first paragraph of runic script
centered between the bottom magic cube and the second paragraph of runic script

symbols.description: A three-pronged (Y-shaped) glyph in the constructed script
symbols.position: throughout the runic paragraphs, repeated many times

symbols.description: A circle with a dot inside (⊙)
symbols.position: near the end of each runic paragraph
in the middle of each runic paragraph

symbols.description: Chevron/angle brackets '>' and '<' combined as doubled/tripled pairs (e.g. ≫, ≪)
symbols.position: near the end of each runic paragraph

symbols.description: Hebrew letters in red: ו (vav), ה (he), ד (dalet), ב (bet), ר (resh), י (yod), ם (final mem), forming 'והדברים'
symbols.position: as red labels between the cube and text

decorations.description: Upper 3x3x3 magic cube drawn in isometric projection; each face is a 3x3 grid of cells containing a number. Outer cells are connected by dashed vertical lines to the corresponding cells in the middle and bottom layers. Magic sum of every row, column, pillar and space diagonal is 666.
components: 27 cells arranged in 3 horizontal layers of 3x3
dashed vertical edges linking the three layers

decorations.description: Lower 3x3x3 magic cube drawn in isometric projection, same style as the upper one. Magic sum is also 666.
components: 27 cells arranged in 3 horizontal layers of 3x3
dashed vertical edges linking the three layers

uncertain_or_unreadable: The exact transcription of the constructed geometric/runic script could not be performed; individual glyphs are reported only by shape category.
Whether the two paragraphs of runic text are the same sentence or two different ones is unclear from the image alone.
